reason: The only residual finding is missing hints for generic attribute names (`file`, `dir`, `class`). All corresponding element names (`import`, `config-file`, `component`) are already in hints, and users searching for these elements would use element-level terms. The hints list is comprehensive. The section content is complete and accurate. Low impact on retrieval quality.

findings_assessment.justification: The missing terms (`file`, `dir`, `class`) are generic attribute names. Their parent elements (`import`, `config-file`, `component`) are already present in hints, so users searching for how to specify files or define components would still retrieve this file. Users rarely search by raw attribute name alone. Phase E did not apply the fix — the hints array in the Phase E output is identical to Phase D — but the practical retrieval impact is negligible given the existing element-name coverage.
